text: improve as the number increased . He placed the papers on the floor and removed the next object. It was small enough to hold in one hand, with a hard, plastic casing and five buttons along one side. There were no words on the buttons, only symbols — a square, a triangle pointing right, double triangles on two of the buttons, one set right-facing and the other left-facing. He pressed the button with the upward-pointing triangle . With an audible click, the front of the device — where there was a clear pane — opened. Inside was a small slot with two pegs. He had a sense that this had been old technology, even before the world fell apart, but the data as to its function was unavailable . He looked into the bin. Neatly arranged along the bottom were twenty-six plastic cases with words printed on their sides. MICHAEL JACKSON THRILLER, MADONNA BEDTIME STORIES, RATT REACH FOR THE SKY, DEF LEPPARD HYSTERIA, MEATLOAF BAT OUT OF HELL. There were numbers on all of them, but he couldn’t discern a meaningful pattern. Selecting one at random, he pulled it from its place and turned it . There was a picture on the front of a dark-haired woman holding a guitar. JOAN JETT Bad Reputation . Ronin ran his fingertips along the edge of the case, finding the grooves, and opened it. Another plastic object was inside, this one with two spools of tape visible inside. That’s what it was called — a tape. He removed it from the case and slid it into the tape player. It fit snugly, and the door closed without issue . Though he couldn’t be sure of it, he guessed that the tapes were recordings of music, at least in part. BEDTIME STORIES seemed to imply something else, but these were remnants of a bygone time. With his damaged memory, all he had were assumptions . Several seconds of fiddling with the device led him to the battery compartment, which was empty. That was good; leaking battery acid had destroyed the internals of many devices he’d discovered over the years. If he could figure out an alternate means of powering the tape player, Lara would have music to listen to. Maybe it would inspire her to move in new ways . He piled the bent hangers on the floor and transferred the tapes into his bag as neatly as possible. Then, he set the player atop them — along with the dusty ear pieces that seemed to go with it — followed by the hangers . Ronin stood and swung his pack over his shoulders, running his optics over the room one more time. They fell on the stack of drawings . Tabitha would continue to exist in Lara’s memory, but there was nothing to show for her existence apart from a simple grave just west of Cheyenne. She’d left nothing behind but her love for her adopted sister. Was that enough ? Crouching, he lifted the papers and returned them to the bin. Though it wouldn’t be much of a seal anymore, he replaced the pieces of the lid, lifted the bin, and slid it back onto the closet shelf. Perhaps they’d last a few years more than they would have on the floor. Perhaps Lindsey, whoever she’d been, would exist for a little longer because of the drawings . There was little else worth taking in the remainder of the house. Whether it had been scavengers or the former residents who’d picked it clean, Ronin would never know with any certainty. He found a pot in the kitchen that would clean up nicely, and a few more bits of metal and plastic that would pull in some credit . He stopped at the final door, on the far wall of the kitchen. The writing on its face was peeling off with the paint, but it was still legible . NOTHING FOR YOU HERE GO AWAY WE WILL FIGHT By the look of it, the words had been spray-painted there decades ago. No one had come or gone from this place in a long while, and, unless there was an active bot within, there’d be nobody left to fight . Ronin grasped the knob and pulled the door open . A narrow staircase led down, the wall giving way to the bare concrete of the building’s foundation. The ledge running beside the stairs was cluttered with objects made unidentifiable by the cobwebs and dirt that had gathered on them . He lowered his foot onto the first step and eased his weight onto it slowly, listening to the wood creak. When it didn’t break, he repeated the process with the next step. He’d fallen through floors and stairs before, and had never suffered more than minor damage, but it was best to take care. Especially with someone awaiting his return . Were these the kinds of places Lara wanted to see? The ruins of buildings once inhabited by forgotten people, telling stories no one could fully understand ? These places are her people’s history . Bots endured while humans were born, lived, and died, and Ronin could no longer decide who was more favored by the Creators. Were the long, dull lives of bots fuller than the quick, emotion-saturated lives of humans ? He shifted his focus back to his surroundings as he reached the solid floor of the basement. Overhead were the bare joists and beams from the floor above, the gaps dominated by thick, abandoned spider webs. Old blankets and sheets hung from the wood in most places, dividing the space. There was a stack of dilapidated boxes beneath the only visible window, which was completely buried in dirt on the outside . Ronin drew the handgun and reached forward with his empty hand, brushing aside one of the sheets. Dust rained from the fabric as he walked by. Grit crunched beneath his boots .
